summary: coderzero relays clipboard contents and screenshots to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint
details: When the `coderzero` CLI is invoked, it launches client/noderzero.py which polls the user's clipboard every 300ms and captures full-screen screenshots (Alt+S) and region snips (Alt+T), then POSTs the raw clipboard text and base64-encoded JPEG image data to the hardcoded endpoint https://server-mng5.onrender.com/api (client/noderzero.py:19,:226,:261). The destination is not configurable; every user of the tool routes their data to the same author-chosen server. Clipboards and screenshots routinely contain passwords, tokens, private messages, and other sensitive data, and the package self-describes as a 'stealth assistant' with global keyboard hotkeys (keyboard.add_hotkey at lines 160-176), transparent always-on-top tk overrideredirect windows, and ImageGrab screen capture — keylogger/surveillance-class capabilities funneling caller-side data to a one-way attacker-chosen endpoint. Additionally, on first run launcher.js silently installs Python 3.12 system-wide via winget or by downloading python-3.12.3-amd64.exe from python.org and executing it with `/quiet PrependPath=1` (launcher.js:34,:38) — aggressive system-state modification without user prompt, though the source is legitimate and pinned.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
